name: 35 Tapes - Album Release at MIR
description:
35 Tapes is an Oslo-based progressive rock band formed by experienced musicians from the Norwegian music scene, united by a shared love of classic progressive rock and atmospheric art rock. Their music blends Mellotron textures, analog synthesizers, layered guitars, and cinematic songwriting into immersive compositions that move between calm introspection and powerful climaxes. 


Band members:

- Jarle Wangen: bass, guitars, vocals
- Morten Lund: guitars, keyboards, vocals (producer)
- Jo Wang: keyboards, Therevox
- Andreas Eriksen: keyboards, percussion
- Kai Lundewall: drums, vocals
- Frank Lewis: live sound, recording engineer, guitar amps, circuit wizard

The band has received attention in the international prog scene. Prog Magazine previously highlighted the band’s debut, while reviews have compared their sound to artists like Genesis, Camel, Marillion and Talk Talk.

Germany’s Eclipsed Magazine described their music as carrying “wonderfully warm, sometimes dark sounds… not virtuosity for the sake of it, but an ethos similar to Pink Floyd, aiming to capture the right moods.” 

Other reviewers have praised their lush and atmospheric approach to progressive rock, noting how their music combines classic prog influences with a modern Scandinavian tone and emotional depth.


About the album – Veil on Life

Veil on Life is the newest chapter in the evolving sound of 35 Tapes. Continuing the band’s fascination with atmosphere, storytelling, and sonic landscapes, the album explores themes of perception, memory, and the fragile layers that shape our experience of the world.

Across expansive compositions and intimate moments, the music moves through shifting textures and moods, balancing analog warmth with modern production. The album invites listeners into a reflective and cinematic journey where melody, ambience, and progressive structures intertwine.



About the concert

Join us at MIR in Oslo for the official release concert of Veil on Life. On this special evening, 35 Tapes will perform music from the new album live for the first time.

Expect an immersive concert experience filled with atmospheric soundscapes, analog textures, and the exploratory spirit of progressive rock, performed in the intimate setting of MIR.

Whether you’ve followed the band from the beginning or are discovering them for the first time, this night celebrates the unveiling of a new musical chapter.

More details about schedule, tickets, and support acts coming soon.


PHOTO: ANNE MARIE FORKER
